example: The witch muttered an incantation over the bubbling cauldron.
mnemonic: in(안에) + cant(노래하다, 라틴어 cantare) + ation = 마법의 노래 → 주문. cant(노래): chant, recant, canticle

example: The bellicose rhetoric between the nations heightened fears of war.
mnemonic: belli(전쟁, 라틴어 bellum) + cose = 전쟁을 좋아하는 → 호전적인. bell(전쟁): belligerent, rebel, antebellum

example: The deterioration of air quality in the city has alarmed health experts.
mnemonic: deterior(더 나쁜, 라틴어) + ation = 더 나빠지는 것 → 악화. deteriorate(악화하다)의 명사형

example: Millions of people live in abject poverty around the world.
mnemonic: ab(아래로) + ject(던지다) = 아래로 던져진 → 비참한. ject(던지다): reject, inject, project, eject

example: The retiring professor received encomiums from colleagues around the world.
mnemonic: en(안에) + com(축제, 그리스어 komos) + ium = 축제에서의 칭송 → 찬사

example: The professor compiled a compendium of essential research papers.
mnemonic: com(함께) + pend(달다, 무게 재다) + ium = 함께 달아 모은 것 → 요약집. pend(달다): pendant, depend, suspend

example: The tech industry has burgeoned in the last decade.
mnemonic: 고대 프랑스어 borjon(싹, 봉오리)에서 유래. 싹이 트다 → 급성장하다

example: He had a morbid fascination with horror movies and ghost stories.
mnemonic: morb(병, 라틴어 morbus) + id = 병의 → 병적인. morb(병): morbidity, comorbidity

example: Libertarian philosophy advocates for minimal government intervention.
mnemonic: libert(자유, 라틴어 libertas) + arian = 자유를 최우선시하는. liber(자유): liberty, liberal, liberate

example: The customer launched into a tirade about the poor service at the restaurant.
mnemonic: 이탈리아어 tirata(사격, 연발), tirare(당기다)에서 유래. 연발 사격처럼 퍼붓는 → 장광설

example: Their relationship remained purely platonic throughout the years.
mnemonic: Plato(플라톤)에서 유래. 플라톤의 이상적 사랑처럼 → 정신적인, 육체적이지 않은

example: The beleaguered company faced lawsuits from multiple directions simultaneously.
mnemonic: be(주위에) + leaguer(진영, 네덜란드어 leger=캠프) = 주위에 진을 치다 → 포위하다. leger(캠프): lager(맥주=군 저장소에서 유래)

example: The scientist debunked the myth that vaccines cause autism.
mnemonic: de(제거) + bunk(허튼소리) = 허튼소리를 제거하다 → 폭로하다, 반박하다

example: Several factors militate against the success of the new policy.
mnemonic: milit(군인, 라틴어 miles) + ate = 군인처럼 싸우다 → (against와 함께) 불리하게 작용하다. military(군사의)

example: The article was full of innuendo suggesting the politician was corrupt.
mnemonic: 라틴어 innuendo(고개를 끄덕이며), in(향해) + nuere(끄덕이다). 고개짓으로 암시하다 → 빗대어 말하기

example: The robin is often considered a harbinger of spring.
mnemonic: '하빈저' → 'harbor(항구)+inger' 항구에 먼저 도착하는 전조!

example: The tortuous mountain road made the journey twice as long.
mnemonic: tort(비틀다, 라틴어 torquere) + uous = 비틀린 → 꾸불꾸불한. tort(비틀다): torture, contort, distort, extort

example: The poet wrote in the local vernacular rather than in formal literary language.
mnemonic: vernacul(집에서 태어난, 라틴어 verna=집에서 태어난 노예) + ar = 집에서 쓰는 말 → 토착어

example: The deep-sea denizens have adapted to extreme pressure and darkness.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 deinz(안의, 라틴어 de intus=안에서) + en = 안에 사는 자 → 거주자

example: The astute businessman spotted the investment opportunity before anyone else.
mnemonic: 라틴어 astutus(교활한, astus=기술에서). 기술이 뛰어난 → 기민한, 빈틈없는

example: Darwin's seminal work on evolution transformed the biological sciences.
mnemonic: semin(씨앗, 라틴어 semen) + al = 씨앗이 되는 → 영향력 있는, 혁신적인. semin(씨앗): disseminate, seminary

example: She was so engrossed in the novel that she forgot to eat dinner.
mnemonic: en(안에) + gross(큰, 라틴어 grossus) = 크게 안에 빠져들다 → 몰두하게 하다

example: The researcher maintained scrupulous attention to detail throughout the study.
mnemonic: scrupul(작은 돌, 라틴어 scrupulus) + ous = 신발 속 작은 돌처럼 마음에 걸리는 → 양심적인

example: The constitution declares certain human rights to be inviolable.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + viol(침범하다) + able = 침범할 수 없는 → 신성불가침의. viol(침범): violate, violence

example: The reclusive author rarely gave interviews or made public appearances.
mnemonic: re(뒤로) + clus(닫다, 라틴어 claudere) + ive = 뒤로 물러나 문을 닫는 → 은둔적인. recluse(은둔자)의 형용사형

example: The film appeals to cerebral viewers who enjoy complex narratives.
mnemonic: cerebr(뇌, 라틴어 cerebrum) + al = 뇌의 → 지적인. cerebrum(대뇌)

example: The reform-minded bureaucrat pushed for modernization of government systems.
mnemonic: bureau(사무실, 프랑스어) + crat(통치자) = 사무실에서 통치하는 사람 → 관료. crat(통치): democrat, aristocrat

example: The caterpillar undergoes a metamorphosis to become a butterfly.
mnemonic: meta(변화) + morph(형태, 그리스어 morphe) + osis = 형태의 변화 → 변태, 변신. morph(형태): amorphous, morphology

example: The credulous tourist was easily deceived by the street scammer.
mnemonic: cred(믿다, 라틴어 credere) + ulous = 너무 쉽게 믿는 → 잘 속는. cred(믿다): credit, credible, incredible, creed

example: He was being deliberately obtuse, pretending not to understand.
mnemonic: ob(향해) + tuse(두드리다) = 두들겨서 무뎌진 → 둔한. 둔각(obtuse angle)도 같은 단어

example: No one could gainsay the effectiveness of the new treatment.
mnemonic: gain(반대, 고대 영어 gegn) + say(말하다) = 반대로 말하다 → 부인하다, 반박하다

example: The magician performed incredible feats of legerdemain on stage.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 léger de main(손이 가벼운). 가벼운 손놀림 → 마술, 교묘한 속임수

example: A fusillade of questions greeted the CEO at the press conference.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 fusiller(총살하다), fusil(총). 총을 일제히 쏘다 → 일제 사격

example: The ceremony was designed to exalt the achievements of the award winners.
mnemonic: ex(위로) + alt(높은, 라틴어 altus) = 높이 올리다 → 칭송하다. alt(높은): altitude, altar, exalted

example: He disparaged her achievements out of jealousy.
mnemonic: dis(부정) + par(동등한, 라틴어 par) + age = 동등하지 않다고 하다 → 폄하하다. par(동등): pair, compare, parity

example: The bombing was carried out in reprisal for the earlier attack.
mnemonic: re(되돌려) + pris(잡다, 라틴어 prehendere) + al = 되잡아주다 → 보복. pris(잡다): prison, surprise, comprise

example: The project received the imprimatur of the board of directors.
mnemonic: 라틴어 imprimatur(인쇄하게 하라). im(안에) + prim(찍다) = 허가 도장을 찍다 → 공식 허가. print(인쇄)와 같은 어원

example: The dilapidated building was scheduled for demolition next month.
mnemonic: di(떨어져) + lapid(돌, 라틴어 lapis) + ated = 돌이 떨어져 나간 → 허물어진. lapid(돌): lapis lazuli, lapidary

example: Her business acumen helped the company grow rapidly.
mnemonic: acu(날카로운, 라틴어 acuere) + men = 날카로운 정신 → 통찰력. acu(날카로운): acute, acupuncture(침술)

example: His ostentatious display of wealth made others uncomfortable.
mnemonic: ostent(보여주다, 라틴어 ostendere) + atious = 과도하게 보여주는 → 과시하는

example: The scientific apparatus in the laboratory was state-of-the-art.
mnemonic: ap(~을 향해) + par(준비하다, 라틴어 parare) + atus = 준비된 것 → 장치. par(준비): prepare, repair, separate

example: The new department subsumed the functions of three smaller divisions.
mnemonic: sub(아래에) + sume(취하다, 라틴어 sumere) = 아래에 취하다 → 포함시키다. sume(취하다): assume, consume, presume

example: The munificent donation funded an entire wing of the new hospital.
mnemonic: muni(선물, 라틴어 munus) + fic(만들다) + ent = 선물을 만드는 → 관대한

example: The team was ebullient after winning the championship game.
mnemonic: e(밖으로) + bull(끓다, 라틴어 bullire) + ient = 끓어 넘치는 → 열광적인. bull(끓다): boil, bulletin

example: The evidence found at the scene incriminated the prime suspect.
mnemonic: in(안에) + crimin(범죄, 라틴어 crimen) + ate = 범죄 안에 넣다 → 유죄를 보여주다. crimin(범죄): crime, criminal, recrimination

example: The truculent gang terrorized the neighborhood for months.
mnemonic: truc(사나운, 라틴어 trux) + ulent = 사나운 → 호전적인, 잔인한

example: Friends gathered to commiserate with her after the loss of her pet.
mnemonic: com(함께) + miser(불쌍한, 라틴어) + ate = 함께 불쌍해하다 → 동정하다. miser(불쌍한): miserable, misery

example: The situation warrants immediate action from the authorities.
mnemonic: warrant(보증, 게르만어 *warand=보호하는 자). 보증하다 → 정당화하다

example: The mayor was removed from office for malfeasance.
mnemonic: mal(나쁜, 라틴어 malus) + feas(하다, 라틴어 facere) + ance = 나쁜 짓을 함 → 비리. mal(나쁜): malice. fac(하다): feasible

example: Critics lauded the director's bold artistic choices in the film.
mnemonic: 라틴어 laudare(칭찬하다), laus(칭찬). 칭찬하다 → 찬양하다. laud(칭찬): laudable, laudatory, applaud
